title: Bart To The Future

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes or gets into some mishap (tires screeching)
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation and scream
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated character moment but not particularly original

jokes.text: Let's see. Judging from the bug buildup, we're gettin' close.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks how much farther to the campground
jokes.punchline: Homer uses bug splatter on windshield as navigation method
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor showing Homer's unconventional logic

jokes.text: The bugs are firmly in charge. They've taken the Visitors Center! They-They ate the comment book!
jokes.setup: Warning about mosquito problems at the lake
jokes.punchline: Escalating from bug problem to bugs literally taking over facilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd visual of bugs eating a comment book

jokes.text: Hey! Hey! My class ring!
jokes.setup: Ranger being attacked by bugs while warning the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: His specific concern about losing his class ring during the attack
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail showing what matters to him even during crisis

jokes.text: Hey! An Indian casino! God bless Native America!
jokes.setup: Family forced to leave campground due to bugs
jokes.punchline: Homer's instant enthusiasm for gambling opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - instant pivot to self-interest

jokes.text: Well, what better place to celebrate your recovery than amidst the frenzied excitement of the casino floor?
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely backwards logic about recovery
jokes.explanation: Great ironic logic from Homer, perfectly captures his obliviousness

jokes.text: Although they seem strange to us, we must respect the ways of the Indian.
jokes.setup: Bart being denied entry to casino as minor
jokes.punchline: Homer's fake noble tone about respecting Native customs when it serves his purpose
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of fake respect when convenient, great Homer hypocrisy

jokes.text: Hi, how are ya? Hi, how are ya? Hi, how are ya? Hi, how are ya?
jokes.punchline: Mindless repetitive greeting showing casino fakeness
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about casino atmosphere, serviceable but not special

jokes.text: The Great Gabbo demands a free night in the presidential suite while performing. We'll give you a free pass to the pasta bar. The Great Gabbo accepts your terms.
jokes.punchline: Immediate acceptance of much lesser offer
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expectations, shows performer's desperation

jokes.text: Gabbo! You've become a real boy! All right! Time for some underage gambling. So long, sucker!
jokes.setup: Bart hiding in ventriloquist dummy box
jokes.punchline: Reveals his scheme while immediately announcing illegal intent
jokes.explanation: Great Bart moment - clever plan execution with typical reckless announcement

jokes.text: Your linen service has broken many promises to us. Laundry bill soar like eagle. Jerk.
jokes.setup: Native American casino chief speaking to Bart
jokes.punchline: Mixing poetic metaphor with blunt insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of elevated speech with casual insult

jokes.text: I wasn't gonna gamble. I just wanted a Bloody Mary.
jokes.punchline: Claiming innocent intent while naming alcoholic drink
jokes.explanation: Good Bart logic - replacing one illegal activity with another

jokes.text: Your father just took out a second mortgage downstairs. You're listed as collateral.
jokes.setup: Bart asking how the chief knows his name
jokes.punchline: Homer has literally bet his son
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant Homer characterization - would absolutely bet his own child

jokes.text: That's Crazy Talk. No, it's true. No, I know. That's my brother. Crazy Talk. We're all a little worried about him.
jokes.setup: Bart says chief bought firecracker from 'a guy' on reservation
jokes.punchline: Crazy Talk is literally someone's name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection turning common expression into character name

jokes.text: Oh, way to make a guitar, Sears.
jokes.punchline: Blaming the store rather than his own negligence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing future Bart's refusal to take responsibility

jokes.text: I'm sick of having to dry myself with a newspaper!
jokes.setup: Bart asks Ralph to use old towel
jokes.punchline: Reveals their living situation is even worse than shown
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing the pathetic living conditions

jokes.text: You mean the spider bite at Disneyland? Or the incident with the over salted fries.
jokes.punchline: Multiple frivolous lawsuits of decreasing severity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's litigious future character

jokes.text: But I can only pay you in popcorn shrimp. Smell ya later!
jokes.setup: Nelson offering the band a gig
jokes.punchline: Payment in food instead of money
jokes.explanation: Good future dystopia detail, Nelson as sleazy club owner works

jokes.text: I can't believe 'Smell ya later' replaced 'Good-bye.' Smell ya later! Smell ya later.
jokes.punchline: Commentary on how language evolves, then immediate adoption
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on linguistic evolution with immediate hypocrisy

jokes.text: Should we take the hover bus or the non-hover bus? Non-hover.
jokes.punchline: Choosing inferior option despite availability of better one
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's continued poor choices

jokes.text: Bleh! This virtual fudge tastes like crap. Mmm. Not half as good as cyber-fudge.
jokes.punchline: Complaining about fake food while comparing to other fake food
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist future gag with typical Homer food obsession

jokes.text: Oh, what a bleak, horrible future we live in! Don't you mean 'present'? Right, right. Present.
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about time perspective
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about time travel narrative structure

jokes.text: Isn't that made of people? Oh, here we go.
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive reaction to valid concern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's casual dismissal of problems

jokes.text: She's president of the United States! President 'elect.' I could've been president, but I'm too real.
jokes.punchline: Bart's delusional self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart delusion - blaming authenticity for his failures

jokes.text: People can't deal with what I'm laying down. They're just, like, 'Whoa, dude. You can't say that.' And I'm, like, 'Watch me.'
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his failed political aspirations
jokes.punchline: Generic rebellious posturing as political philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Bart's shallow understanding of politics

jokes.text: You should've thought of that before you dropped out of the DeVry Institute.
jokes.punchline: Even failing at a for-profit college
jokes.explanation: Good detail about Bart's educational failures, DeVry reference adds specificity

jokes.text: What happened to you, man? You used to be cool. I'm still cool! Nah. You've changed, man. Well, I do have this robotic prostate. But you can't see it. Oh, you can.
jokes.setup: Bart saying Homer isn't cool anymore
jokes.punchline: Homer's visible robotic prostate
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect visual punchline about aging

jokes.text: Jesus, is that you?
jokes.punchline: Flanders presumably has long hair/beard from blindness
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag playing on Flanders' appearance and religiousness

jokes.text: Oh, it was great at first. But, you know, at the 10-year mark, your eyes fall out.
jokes.punchline: Extreme long-term consequences of trendy medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant commentary on medical fads and their unforeseen consequences

jokes.text: But only because you haven't outed Rod and Todd.
jokes.punchline: Blackmail prevention regarding his sons' sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist on Flanders character, implies complex future dynamics

jokes.text: Now we'd like to play a Jimmy Buffett song but he uncooly charges people to cover them. So here's a Captain Bart original. 'Wasted once more in Daiquiritaville'
jokes.punchline: Obvious ripoff of Margaritaville with thin justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart move - blatant plagiarism while claiming moral high ground

jokes.text: We need more power! The shield won't hold much longer!
jokes.punchline: Band apparently has force field technology for protection
jokes.explanation: Creative future gag - even terrible musicians have defensive technology

jokes.text: Oh, no. I've got half a beer in there, and Bewitched is on.
jokes.punchline: Priorities focused on immediate comfort over major loss
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's inability to grasp serious consequences

jokes.text: Damn! I was so close! I had 327!
jokes.punchline: Bart thinking 327 was close to 446
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart math logic - shows his fundamental misunderstanding of probability

jokes.text: So I'm the president's no-good brother. Moochie-moochie.
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to exploitation opportunity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bart immediately sees angle for personal gain

jokes.text: Yes, I am proud to be America's first straight female president.
jokes.punchline: Implies all previous female presidents were gay
jokes.explanation: Clever implication about future political landscape, economical delivery

jokes.text: As you know, we've inherited quite a budget crunch from President Trump.
jokes.punchline: Trump as previous president (2000 seemed impossible then)
jokes.explanation: Accidentally prophetic joke that became cultural touchstone - amazing in retrospect

jokes.text: The Balanced Breakfast Program just created a generation of ultra-strong super criminals. And Midnight Basketball taught them to function without sleep.
jokes.punchline: Beneficial programs having opposite, extreme effects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on unintended consequences of social programs

jokes.text: And Midnight Basketball taught them to function without sleep.
jokes.punchline: Recreation program gave criminals superhuman abilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation continuing the absurd logic of the previous joke

jokes.text: I knew you'd need some help keeping it real so I figured I could be like your co-president.
jokes.setup: Bart showing up at White House
jokes.punchline: Completely delusional understanding of government
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart delusion - thinks he can just declare himself co-president

jokes.text: Mom, Lisa won't share! Be nice to your brother, Lisa.
jokes.punchline: Reverting to childhood sibling dynamics
jokes.explanation: Excellent regression to childhood dynamics despite adult circumstances

jokes.text: Aw, just like her mommy, Maggie Senior.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused naming convention
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion about naming, shows his mental limitations

jokes.text: Dad, that's just a myth. Lincoln didn't hide any gold in the White House. Then what is his ghost protecting?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining Lincoln's gold is a myth
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption that Lincoln's ghost exists and is protecting something
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - accepts ghost as fact while questioning treasure

jokes.text: I fell out two times.
jokes.setup: Bart mentioning helicopter ride for Ralph
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of multiple helicopter ejections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph understatement about clearly dangerous situation

jokes.text: Yeah. But they said I wouldn't get my first check for two weeks. Meanwhile, they're making major interest on my salary.
jokes.punchline: Conspiracy theory about standard pay period
jokes.explanation: Great Bart paranoia about normal business practices, perfect character moment

jokes.text: I started what from a what? Your plan makes no sense.
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete confusion at basic vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stupidity moment, shows intellectual decline with age

jokes.text: Gold bars discovered by Marge? Zero! Gold bars discovered by Homer? Well, let's just see.
jokes.setup: Homer keeping score of treasure hunting
jokes.punchline: Competitive attitude about imaginary treasure hunt
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's need to compete even in futility

jokes.text: We could call it 'a temporary refund adjustment.' I love it. Really? What else do you love, Lisa? Fiscal solvency.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's unsexy passion for responsible government
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - finding bureaucracy romantic

jokes.text: I want you to play my demo tape in the background while you're yakkin' about whatever.
jokes.setup: Lisa about to give presidential address
jokes.punchline: Bart's complete disrespect for importance of moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart selfishness - using sister's presidential moment for personal gain

jokes.text: Refund? Hey, sounds good to me. Sure beats a tax. We love you, President Simpson!
jokes.setup: Lisa using euphemism for tax increase
jokes.punchline: Public falling for obvious word manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about public susceptibility to euphemisms

jokes.text: but it is only through arduousity that- If you like refund adjustments and the music I play
jokes.punchline: Made-up word interrupted by terrible song
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Lisa's made-up word and Bart's interference

jokes.text: Tax hike! Hold the phone, Mabel! I never trusted her. Don't blame me, I voted for Chastity Bono!
jokes.setup: Bart revealing it's actually a tax
jokes.punchline: Random voter claiming they voted for unlikely candidate
jokes.explanation: Decent political humor with absurd alternative candidate reference

jokes.text: We don't own the shores of Tripoli. Shh! By the time they find out, we'll have taken our cyanide pills.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what to give foreign creditors
jokes.punchline: Plan involves fraud and mass suicide
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation of government desperation

jokes.text: Come on. Every president gets three secret murders. If you don't use them by the end of the term, phht! They're gone.
jokes.punchline: Presidential murders as standard benefit with expiration
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant dark comedy treating murder as bureaucratic perk

jokes.text: Of what? The United States of Dorksylvania? Don't leave me hanging, Greenspan!
jokes.punchline: Childish wordplay followed by appeal to Federal Reserve chair
jokes.explanation: Good Bart juvenile humor with unexpected economic figure reference

jokes.text: They couldn't keep pants on Kissinger.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about skinny-dipping at Camp David
jokes.punchline: Reference to Kissinger's alleged nudity at presidential retreat
jokes.explanation: Excellent unexpected historical reference with vivid mental image

jokes.text: What's the difference between Pakistan and a pancake? I don't know any pancakes that were nuked by India! What? Too soon?
jokes.punchline: Geopolitical humor about nuclear warfare
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor from Ralph with self-aware 'too soon' tag

jokes.text: Damn straight. And I'm here to tell ya you've been sent on a wild goose chase. When my brother Jimmy was making peace in the Middle East, he sent me to Belly Flop Academy.
jokes.punchline: Being sent to fake academy during important historical events
jokes.explanation: Creative historical reference with absurd 'Belly Flop Academy'

jokes.text: There's an embarrassment of riches at the Caesar's Pow-Wow Indian Casino! Yeah, Crazy Talk came up with that. He got idea from Dances with Focus Groups.
jokes.punchline: Movie title parodies for marketing concepts
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Crazy Talk with clever movie title parody

jokes.text: My gold is in the heart of every freedom-loving American. It's in our mighty rivers, our majestic- Aw, crap! That lying, rail-splitting, theater-going freak!
jokes.punchline: Homer's angry reaction to poetic metaphor, including theater reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction with brilliant 'theater-going' assassination reference

jokes.text: Guys, the thing is, we totally have the money, and we tried to wire it to you. But you know how banks screw up. We tried to call you all day Saturday. We were there Saturday. Dude, I know, I left a message with some guy named Hans.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate lie using typical customer service excuses
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart bullshitting with believable customer service horror story

jokes.text: I left a message with some guy named Hans. / Hans? / He might've been a temp. Very surly.
jokes.punchline: Makes up German-sounding name, representative takes it seriously
jokes.explanation: Good improvised lie that works because of stereotypical name choice

jokes.text: What happened to you, China? You used to be cool. Hey, China is still cool. You pay later! Later!
jokes.punchline: Country trying to maintain cool reputation while collecting money
jokes.explanation: Great personification of China as peer-pressured teenager

jokes.text: Legalize it. Legalize what? Oh! Oh. Consider it done.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's realization and immediate agreement about drugs
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Lisa's political pragmatism

jokes.text: Hey, this is expired!
jokes.punchline: Even the reward for spiritual journey is defective
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of mystical experience with mundane reality

jokes.text: Dad pushed a waitress, and Mom lost $20,000.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of typical Simpson family disasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect casual delivery of family chaos, very economic

jokes.text: What about me? Eh, some government job.
jokes.punchline: Bart dismissing the presidency as 'some government job'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart understatement and dismissiveness about Lisa's achievement

jokes.text: Moochie-moochie!
jokes.punchline: Return to present Bart already planning to exploit future knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bart learned nothing from the vision

tmdb_overview: We see a possible future in which Bart is a grown-up slacker, living with Ralph Wiggum, while Lisa is no less than President of the United States.
